page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs derived from 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 165,322.49 and a PassMark CPU score of 115,340.92. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 12,789.56 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 597.08 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores falling in the average category. The hardware excels in parallelized tasks, showing top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-192 offers high compute density and memory bandwidth for highly parallelized workloads without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. However, workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage may face resource trade-offs. This instance is optimized for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Running at 2.3 GHz with 96 physical cores, the instance includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. It does not contain local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional throughput for database operations and multi-threaded decompression. Single-core performance and memory scores remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for parallelized, compute-intensive tasks, making it suitable for software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and LLM inference prompt processing.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads.
